response: An important leader of the Democratic-Republican Party was Thomas Jefferson, who formed the party in 1791–93 to oppose the centralizing policies of the Federalist Party. He served as a key figure in the party, and later, James Madison, also a Democratic-Republican, held important positions within the party. Albert Gallatin, a prominent leader of the Democratic-Republican Party, served as the longest-tenured Secretary of the Treasury and was a high-ranking diplomat. Jeremiah Morrow, another Democratic-Republican politician, served as the last Democratic-Republican governor of Ohio.
